intro: 6:15 AM. You're up before the alarm because someone else got up first.

scenarios.title: The Morning Chaos
scenarios.subtitle: Every morning is an event.
scenarios.prompt: Backpacks, lunches, shoes, where are the shoes. Everyone needs to be out the door in 30 minutes.

choices.text: We've got a routine. Everyone knows what to do.
I'm directing traffic the whole time. It works but I'm the engine.
It's a scramble every single day and I don't know how we make it.
My partner handles the morning. I deal with other stuff.

scenarios.title: The Quiet Moment
scenarios.subtitle: Finally.
scenarios.prompt: The house is quiet. Kids are at school. You sit down for the first time today.

choices.text: Open my laptop and start working.
Look at the list of things I've been putting off.
Sit there for a minute. Just a minute.
Start cleaning up the mess from this morning.
choices.reaction: It's not going to clean itself.

scenarios.title: The Mental Load
scenarios.subtitle: The stuff nobody sees.
scenarios.prompt: Your phone buzzes. Permission slip due tomorrow. Doctor appointment to reschedule. Your kid needs something for a project by Wednesday. That was all in the last hour.

choices.text: Add everything to my calendar and lists. It's a lot but it's organized.
It's all in my head. That's terrifying but it's how I operate.
Write it in three different places and hope I check one of them.
choices.reaction: Somewhere in there is the right one.

choices.text: Text my partner. We need to split this up.
choices.reaction: Message sent. Ball's in their court.

scenarios.title: The Scheduling Nightmare
scenarios.subtitle: Nothing lines up.
scenarios.prompt: You're trying to schedule your kid's dentist appointment. The only openings conflict with soccer practice, a work meeting, and pickup time.

choices.text: Rearrange everything else around it. The appointment matters.
Ask my partner if they can handle this one.
Push it to next month. I can't deal with this right now.
choices.reaction: Next month has its own problems.

choices.text: Call back later. I need to look at everyone's schedule first.
choices.reaction: The family calendar holds the answers. Hopefully.

scenarios.title: The Dinner Question
scenarios.subtitle: The question that never stops.
scenarios.prompt: It's 4pm. You're staring at the fridge. Someone is about to ask what's for dinner.

choices.text: Already planned. Meals are set for the week.
I've got three go-to meals. I'll figure it out in the next hour.
I dread this moment every single day.
Open a delivery app. Not tonight.

scenarios.title: The Kid Moment
scenarios.subtitle: They need you right now.
scenarios.prompt: After dinner. Your kid comes to you with a homework problem. You have no idea how to help.

choices.text: Sit with them and figure it out together. Google if I have to.
choices.reaction: Two heads. One very confusing worksheet.

choices.text: Listen and try my best even if I'm not sure what to say.
Tell them to ask their teacher tomorrow.
I want to help but I'm running on empty right now.

scenarios.title: The Household Admin
scenarios.subtitle: The never-ending list.
scenarios.prompt: There's a bill you forgot to pay, a leak you've been ignoring, and an insurance thing you need to call about. It's all been sitting there for weeks.

choices.text: Sit down and knock them all out at once.
Handle the most urgent one and ignore the rest.
My partner deals with most of this stuff.
Add them back to the list. I'll get to it eventually.

scenarios.title: The Late Night
scenarios.subtitle: When it's just you.
scenarios.prompt: It's late. Everyone's asleep. You're lying there and your brain won't shut off.

choices.text: Thinking about tomorrow's schedule. Making a mental list.
Worrying about money. It's always in the background.
Wondering if I'm doing enough for my kids.
choices.reaction: You're thinking about it. That says something.

choices.text: Actually feeling pretty good. Today was a good day.
